title: Low-power wireless device for asset-integrity monitoring
abstract: A sensor for ultrasonically measuring a portion of a structure, the sensor comprising: (a) a transducer for converting an analog transmit signal to an ultrasonic transmit signal, and for converting an ultrasonic reflected signal to an analog reflected signal; (b) a housing connected to the transducer; (c) a connection mechanism for permanently installing the transductor on the structure; (d) electrical components contained within the housing, the electrical components comprising at least: a processor having an integrated A/D converter and configured for Equivalent-Time Sampling (ETS) analog reflected signals from the transducer to obtain a digitized composite signal, processing the digitized composite reflected signal to generate an A-scan signal; and transmitting the data signal based on the A-scan signal, wherein the electrical components do not comprise a discrete A/D converter or a field programmable gate array (FPGA).
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. (New) A sensor for ultrasonically measuring a portion of a structure, said sensor comprising: a transducer for converting an analog transmit signal to an ultrasonic transmit signal, and for converting an ultrasonic reflected signal to an analog reflected signal; a housing integrated with said transducer, and comprising a mechanism for securing the sensor to said structure, said housing containing at least: a processor; a transmit and receive circuit for transmitting an analog transmit signal to said transducer in response to a transmit trigger from said processor, and for receiving an analog reflected signal from said transducer; an A/D converter for digitizing only a portion of said analog reflected signal in response to a sample trigger from said processor; a wireless data transmitter for transmitting wirelessly a data signal from said processor; a battery to supply power to said processor, said wireless data transmitter, said transmit and receive circuit, and said A/D converter; memory operatively connected to said processor and configured to instruct said processor to execute the following steps: repeatedly triggering said transmit and receive circuit and said A/D converter to obtain a digitized composite signal through time- equivalent sampling; processing said digitized composite reflected signal to generate an A- scan signal; and wirelessly transmitting said data signal based on said A-scan signal for transmission to a discrete collection device. 2. (New) The sensor of claim 1 , wherein said A/D converted is integrated with said processor. 3. (New) The sensor of claim 2, wherein said sensor does not comprise a discrete A/D converter and a field programmable gate array (FPGA). 4. (New) The sensor of claim 3, wherein said sensor is characterized by a total capacitance, wherein said total capacitance is lower than a sensor having a discrete A/D converter and a FPGA. 5. (New) The sensor of claim 3, wherein said housing is characterized by a total capacitance and operating voltage, which qualifies as being intrinsically safe. 6. (New) The sensor of claim 1 , wherein said battery is a small, low capacity battery. 7. (New) The sensor of claim 6, wherein said battery is one of the following sizes: 123, AA, C, D. 8. (New) The sensor of claim 6, wherein said battery comprises two of the following sizes: 123, AA, C, D. 9. (New) The sensor of claim 1, wherein said processor is instructed to configure said sensor in a low-power state between readings. 10. (New) The sensor of claim 9, wherein said processor is instructed to wake up from a low power state to measure said structure on a predetermined interval. 11. (New) The sensor of claim 10, where said predetermined interval is between 1 minute and 1 year. 12. (New) The sensor of claim 1, wherein said triggering comprises: triggering said transmit circuit to transmit a plurality of sequential analog transmit signals to said ultrasonic transducer; 

 triggering said A/D converter to digit a sample of a plurality of sequential reflected signals corresponding to said plurality of sequential transmit signals; wherein either said transmit triggers or said converter triggers are delayed a predetermined time. 13. (New) The sensor of claim 12 wherein each sequential transmit signal is delayed by said predetermined time. 14. (New) The sensor of claim 1 , wherein said A/D converter is configured for X samples per second (SPS), and said composite signal comprises an effective sampling rate of Y samples per second, wherein Y is greater than X. 15. (New) The sensor of claim 14, wherein Y is at least 5 times greater than X. 16. (New) The sensor of claim 15, wherein Y is about 10 times greater than X. 17. (New) The sensor of claim 16, wherein said analog reflected signal has a frequency of F Hz, and wherein Y is at least 2 times F. 18. (New) The sensor of claim 17, wherein Y is at least 5 times F. 19. (New) The sensor of claim 1, wherein said A/D converter and said processor are integrated on a common chip. 20. (New) A system for ultrasonically measuring a portion of a structure, said system comprising: 

 a sensor comprising at least: a transducer for converting an analog transmit signal to an ultrasonic transmit signal, and for converting an ultrasonic reflected signal to an analog reflected signal; a housing integrated with said transducer, and comprising a mechanism for securing the sensor to said structure, and containing at least: a processor; a wireless data transmitter for transmitting wirelessly a data signal from said processor; a transmit and receive circuit for transmitting an analog transmit signal to said transducer in response to a transmit trigger from said processor, and for receiving an analog reflected signal from said transducer; an A/D converter for digitizing only a portion of said analog reflected signal in response to a sample trigger from said processor; a battery to supply power to said processor, said wireless data transmitter, said transmit and receive circuit, and said A/D converter; memory operatively connected to said processor and configured to instruct said processor to execute the following steps: repeatedly triggering said transmit and receive circuit and said A/D converter to obtain a digitized composite signal through time- equivalent sampling; processing said digitized composite reflected signal to generate an A-scan signal; and wirelessly transmitting said data signal based on said A-scan signal to a discrete wireless collection device; and said wireless collection device. 21. (New) The system of claim 20, wherein said wireless collection device comprises a wireless gateway that is connected to a Local Area Network (LAN) and subsequently forwards said data. 22. (New) The system of claim 21, wherein said gateway transmits said data signal across said LAN to a local server. 23. (New) The system of claim 21, wherein said gateway transmits said data signal across said LAN to a remote data server. 24. (New) The system of claim 22 or 23, wherein said server hosts a data viewing application. 25. (New) The system of claim 20, wherein said wireless collection device stores said data signal. 26. (New) The system of claim 25, wherein said wireless collection device stores said data signal until said data signal is collected using a handheld data collection device. 27. (New) The system of claim 26, wherein said handheld data collection device is at least one of a tablet computer, a laptop computer, or a smart phone. 28. (New) The system of claim 26, wherein said handheld data collection device is connected to said wireless data collection device using a cable. 29. (New) The system of claim 26, wherein said handheld data collection device is connected to said wireless data collection device using a wireless connection such as Wi-Fi or Bluetooth. 30. (New) A method of ultrasonically measuring a portion of a structure using a sensor comprising a transducer for converting an analog transmit signal to an ultrasonic transmit signal and for converting an ultrasonic reflected signal to an analog reflected signal, a transmit and receive circuit for transmitting an analog transmit signal to said transducer in response to a transmit trigger from a processor, and for receiving an analog reflected signal 

 from said transducer, and an A/D converter integrated with said processor for digitizing only a sample of said analog reflected signal in response to a sample trigger form said processor, said method comprising: installing said sensor on said structure such that the position of said sensor relative to said structure is fixed; repeatedly triggering said transmit circuit and said A/D converter while said sensor's position to said structure remains fixed to obtain a digitized composite signal through time-equivalent sampling; processing said digitized composite reflected signal to generate an A-scan signal; and wirelessly transmitting said data signal based on said A-scan signal to said transmitted for transmission to a discrete device. 31. (New) The sensor of claim 30, wherein said A/D converted is integrated with said processor. 32. (New) The sensor of claim 31 , wherein said sensor does not comprise a discrete A/D converter and a field programmable gate array (FPGA). 33. (New) The sensor of claim 30, wherein said repeatedly triggering comprises: repeatedly triggering said transmit circuit to transmit a plurality of sequential analog transmit signals to said ultrasonic transducer; repeatedly triggering said A/D converter to digital a sample of a plurality of sequential reflected signals corresponding to said plurality of sequential transmit signals; wherein either the repeated transmit triggers or the repeated converter triggers are delayed a predetermined time. 34. (New) The method of claim 33, wherein each sequential transmit signal is delayed by said predetermined time. 35. (New) The method of claim 30, wherein said A/D converter is configured for X samples per second (SPS), and said composite signal comprises an effective sampling rate of X samples per second, wherein Y is greater than X. 36. (New) The method of claim 35, wherein Y is at least 5 times greater than X. 37. (New) The method of claim 36, wherein Y is about 10 times greater than X. 38. (New) The method of claim 37, wherein said analog reflected signal has a frequency of F Hz, and wherein Y is at least 2 times F. 39. (New) The method of claim 38, wherein Y is at least 5 times F. 40. (New) A sensor for ultrasonically measuring a portion of a structure, said sensor comprising: a transducer for converting an analog transmit signal to an ultrasonic transmit signal, and for converting an ultrasonic reflected signal to an analog reflected signal; a housing connected to said transducer; a connection mechanism for permanently installing said transductor on said structure; electrical components contained within said housing, said electrical components comprising at least: a processor having an integrated A/D converter and configured for Equivalent-Time Sampling (ETS) analog reflected signals from said transducer to obtain a digitized composite signal, processing said digitized composite reflected signal to generate an A-scan signal; and transmitting said data signal based on said A-scan signal, wherein said electrical components do not comprise a discrete A/D converter or a field programmable gate array (FPGA). 41. (New) The sensor of claim 40, wherein said sensor is characterized by a total capacitance, wherein said total capacitance is lower than a sensor having a discrete A/D converter and a FPGA. 42. (New) The sensor of claim 40, wherein said electrical components have a total capacitance and operating voltage, which qualifies as being intrinsically safe.
